subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Markers
- **1 board** — World map with outbreaks track, infection rate track, and cure indicators.
- **1 outbreaks marker** — Tracks outbreak count.
- **1 infection rate marker** — Tracks current infection draw rate.
- **4 cure markers** — One per disease color; each has vial side and eradicated side.

#### Pawns & Roles
- **7 role cards** — Role identities with special abilities.
- **7 pawns** — Matching pawns for role cards.
- **4 reference cards** — Action summary cards for players.

#### Player Deck
- **59 player cards total** — 48 City cards + 6 Epidemic cards + 5 Event cards.

#### Infection System
- **48 infection cards** — City infection deck.
- **96 disease cubes** — 24 cubes in each of 4 colors.

#### Research Stations
- **6 research stations** — Place one in Atlanta during setup.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place board** — Put the board in reach of all players.
2. **Prepare supplies** — Place 6 research stations and separate 96 cubes into 4 color piles.
3. **Atlanta station** — Place 1 research station in Atlanta.
4. **Outbreak marker** — Place on 0 of the Outbreaks Track.
5. **Cure markers** — Place all 4 vial-side-up near cure indicators.
6. **Infection rate marker** — Place on the left-most "2" space.
7. **Seed infections (3-cube step)** — Draw 3 infection cards; place 3 matching cubes on each city.
8. **Seed infections (2-cube step)** — Draw 3 more cards; place 2 matching cubes on each city.
9. **Seed infections (1-cube step)** — Draw 3 more cards; place 1 matching cube on each city.
10. **Discard seeded cards** — Put these 9 cards face up in Infection Discard Pile.
11. **Form Infection Deck** — Remaining infection cards become the draw deck.

- **Setup total** — Place exactly 18 cubes during infection setup.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Setup (All Games)
- **Reference cards** — Give each player 1 reference card.
- **Deal roles** — Shuffle role cards; deal 1 face up to each player.
- **Place pawns** — Put each chosen role’s matching pawn in Atlanta.
- **Remove extras** — Unused roles and pawns leave the game.
- **Prepare player deck base** — Remove all 6 Epidemic cards before dealing starting hands.
- **Shuffle base deck** — Shuffle remaining City + Event cards.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Starting hand size** — Deal 4 cards to each player.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Starting hand size** — Deal 3 cards to each player.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Starting hand size** — Deal 2 cards to each player.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose difficulty** — Use 4, 5, or 6 Epidemic cards.
2. **Remove unused Epidemics** — Return extra Epidemic cards to the box.
3. **Split player cards** — Divide remaining player cards into piles equal to Epidemic count.
4. **Seed each pile** — Shuffle 1 Epidemic card into each pile.
5. **Stack deck** — Stack piles into final Player Deck with smaller piles on bottom.
6. **Check starting hands** — Players may review their City cards.
7. **Pick first player** — Player with highest-population City card starts.
8. **Begin turns** — Start with normal 3-part turn flow.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Do actions** — Perform up to 4 actions.
2. **Draw player cards** — Draw 2 Player cards.
3. **Resolve Epidemics** — Resolve any Epidemic card effects immediately when drawn.
4. **Hand limit check** — If above 7 cards, discard down to 7 or play allowed Event(s).
5. **Infect cities** — Draw infection cards equal to current infection rate; place cubes.
6. **Pass turn** — Player on the left becomes active player.

- **Co-op table rule** — Everyone may advise.
- **Decision authority** — Active player makes final turn decisions.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action Capacity
- **Per turn cap** — You may take up to 4 actions.
- **Repeat allowed** — You may repeat the same action; each use costs 1 action.

#### Movement Actions
- **Drive / Ferry** — Move to an adjacent city connected by a white line.
- **Direct Flight** — Discard a City card to move to that card’s city.
- **Charter Flight** — Discard the City card matching your current city; move to any city.
- **Shuttle Flight** — Move between two cities that both have research stations.
- **Wraparound routes** — Some edge routes connect across map edges (example: Sydney ↔ Los Angeles).

#### Board Actions
- **Build Research Station** — Discard current-city card to place a station there.
- **Station cap rule** — If all 6 are built, move one existing station to the new city.
- **Treat Disease** — Remove 1 cube from your current city.
- **Treat cured disease** — If that color is cured, remove all cubes of that color in your city with one Treat action.

#### Card Exchange Action
- **Share Knowledge (give)** — Give the City card matching your current city to a player in the same city.
- **Share Knowledge (take)** — Take that matching City card from a player in the same city.
- **Consent rule** — Both players must agree to the transfer.

#### Cure Action
- **Discover a Cure (standard)** — At a research station, discard 5 City cards of the same color.
- **Cure marker** — Move that disease marker to its cure indicator.
- **Scientist exception** — Scientist needs only 4 cards of the same color.

#### Discard & Hand Limit Rules
- **Action discards** — Action-related card discards go to Player Discard Pile.
- **Hand overflow** — If a player exceeds 7 cards after receiving a card, immediately discard or play allowed Event(s).

#### Eradication Rules
- **Eradication trigger** — If the last cube of a cured disease is removed, flip marker to eradicated side.
- **Win condition note** — Eradication is helpful but not required to win.
- **Not cured caveat** — Removing the last cube of an uncured disease does not eradicate it.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Supported player counts** — Rulebook setup uses 2, 3, and 4 players.
- **2-player variation found** — Starting hand is 4 cards each.
- **3-player variation found** — Starting hand is 3 cards each.
- **4-player variation found** — Starting hand is 2 cards each.
- **No solo mode text found** — Provided excerpt contains no solo rules.
- **No 5-6 player setup found** — Provided excerpt does not define those counts.

#### Epidemic Sequence (Exact Order)
1. **INCREASE** — Move infection rate marker forward 1 space.
2. **INFECT** — Draw bottom Infection card; place 3 cubes on that city; discard that card.
3. **INTENSIFY** — Shuffle Infection Discard Pile; place it on top of Infection Deck.

#### Outbreak Pressure
- **Loss threshold** — If outbreaks reach 8, team loses immediately.
- **Why it spikes** — Intensify puts recent infection cities back on top of deck.

#### Role Asymmetry
- **Role powers** — Roles modify baseline rules.
- **Scientist example** — Scientist cures with 4 cards instead of 5.

#### Event Cards
- **Timing** — Event cards can be played at any time when card text allows.
- **Use case** — Events are tempo tools for crisis prevention or recovery.
- **Action cost note** — Event timing follows card text; not all event plays consume an action.

#### Table Protocol
- **Open advice** — Team discussion is encouraged.
- **Final call** — Active player decides their turn.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Team victory** — Discover cures for all 4 diseases: Blue, Yellow, Black, and Red.

#### Loss Conditions
- **Outbreak loss** — 8 outbreaks occur.
- **Cube supply loss** — You must place a cube but that color supply is empty.
- **Time loss** — You must draw Player cards but Player Deck is exhausted.

#### Scoring Model
- **No VP track** — Pandemic does not use victory points.
- **No final tally** — Game ends immediately on win/loss trigger.
- **No tiebreaker** — Binary team outcome means no tie-break system.

#### Eradication Clarification
- **Optional milestone** — Eradicating diseases is not required for victory.
- **Practical benefit** — Eradicated diseases no longer place new cubes during infection/epidemic resolution.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Hit red-alert cities first** — Prioritize setup cities with 3 cubes to prevent early outbreaks.
- **Route with purpose** — Build stations to shorten paths between hot zones and cure handoff hubs.
- **Call cure targets early** — Assign first cure colors from opening hands before turn 1 drift starts.
- **Use efficient Treat timing** — Save high-value Treat actions for cured colors and outbreak chokepoints.
- **Protect cure cards** — Avoid burning same-color sets for flights unless crisis prevention is immediate.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Low-risk cleanup obsession** — Clearing safe 1-cube cities while 3-cube cities remain invites chain outbreaks.
- **Travel-card overspending** — Discarding cure-color cards for movement slows cure completion.
- **Random station building** — Building stations without a route plan wastes actions and cards.
- **Event hoarding** — Waiting for perfect Event value often ends with unused cards in a loss.
- **Hand-limit neglect** — Failing to plan around 7-card cap causes forced discard of key cure cards.
- **Eradication tunnel vision** — Chasing full eradication before securing all cures can lose to deck timer.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Contain vs cure push** — Decide whether this round should reduce outbreak risk or complete a cure set.
- **Mobility vs card equity** — Evaluate if flight tempo is worth sacrificing cure-critical City cards.
- **Station placement timing** — Choose whether to build now for long-term route efficiency or act on immediate board fires.
- **Event now vs Event later** — Weigh immediate stabilization against saving Events for future epidemic spikes.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: Pandemic rulebook text provided in prompt (components, setup, action list, epidemic sequence, win/loss conditions).
